purpose: Declares what an agent or human must do on observing a specific class of ambiguity. Taste never resolves ambiguity.

policies.trigger: Two code paths implement the same behavior under different names.
policies.action_detail: Pick the path that matches the domain owner in truth-owners.contract.json. Delete the other. If neither matches the declared owner, halt and escalate.

policies.trigger: A truth has more than one writer.
policies.action_detail: Route all writes through the domain declared in truth-owners.contract.json. Remove shadow writers.

policies.trigger: The same setting exists in two config surfaces.
policies.action_detail: Declare one surface as owner in truth-owners.contract.json. Make the other surface read-only or remove it.

policies.trigger: A field, table, or responsibility has no declared owner.
policies.action_detail: Do not guess. Raise the gap; propose an owner only as a judgment entry in .law/context/current-system.json.

policies.trigger: A rule in doctrine or charter uses soft language ('usually', 'normally', 'should') without an enforcement path.
policies.action_detail: Either add an enforcement path (contract entry, test, review gate) or delete the rule. A soft rule without enforcement is noise.

policies.trigger: Observed runtime behavior contradicts documented behavior.
policies.action_detail: Treat runtime as evidence. Update docs to match observed behavior OR fix runtime to match docs. Declare which; never leave the gap.

policies.trigger: Two plausible pre-implementation designs exist with no existing code, no declared owner in truth-owners.contract.json, and no clear winner from charter, contracts, or doctrine.
policies.action_detail: Build minimal spikes of each design (<= 50 lines each). Record both spikes in .law/context/current-system.json#judgment_log as inferred. Pick the surviving design based on spike evidence. Log the rejected spike with a superseded_by pointer to the winner. Until a spike resolves the fork, record the entry in contradiction_map with class: design-fork and status: open-noted.

policies.trigger: A truth has 3+ candidate writer modules, each with partial evidence. Distinct from unclear-ownership (no candidate identified); here, multiple candidates are real.
policies.action_detail: List every candidate in contradiction_map[].evidence. Declare no owner in truth-owners.contract.json until escalation resolves the choice. Treat reads against the truth as unclear-ownership (blocked). Record the entry in contradiction_map with class: multi-candidate-owner and status: open, one evidence entry per candidate.

policies.trigger: A module reads an owned truth (declared in truth-owners.contract.json) by bypassing the owner's abstraction layer (e.g., direct SQL on the owner's table, direct file read of the owner's config, direct cache access bypassing the owner's cache layer).
policies.action_detail: Apply the shadow-writer discipline (doctrine section 5, multi-writer) to readers. Refuse to add new shadow-reader code. Record every existing shadow-reader in contradiction_map with class: shadow-reader and status: open or open-noted. Require a stabilization plan before any new dependency takes on the shadow-read path.

on_ambiguity_observed: Find the matching policy by trigger
If no policy matches, apply default_action_when_no_policy_matches
Always record the observation in .law/context/current-system.json before acting
